author: wtomin
body: Any versions or minimal versions requirements for `kernels` to work with vllm-omni and verl-omni?

author: wtomin
wtomin
body: I don't quick understand the differences between `version=1`, `version=2`, and default. Can you explain their differences and the fallback logic in the document?

author: RuixiangMa
body: If the hub kernel only provides flash_attn_varlen_func but not flash_attn_func (which the __init__ allows — it only requires one of the two), this would pass None as attn_func and crash when piecewise_attn calls it.

author: RuixiangMa
body: The PR description states FA3 Hub is "optimized for Hopper+ SM90 architectures," but  doesn't gate FLASH_ATTN_3_HUB on SM90+？

author: RuixiangMa
body: the try/except around kernel loading just print()s a message and continues. The test passes even if the hub kernel can't be loaded ( package not installed, etc.). This means CI shows green without actually testing the hub kernels.

author: yenuo26
body: In practice, this kind of skipif can easily go unnoticed — if the resources are misconfigured by accident, no one will realize that the test case was skipped and not actually executed. Since you have already added the hardware marker, I would suggest removing this skipif.

author: yenuo26
body: I believe that since the purpose of this test case is specifically to verify this functionality, it should perform strict validation here, rather than using try-except, which could cause a false pass in the CI.

author: SamitHuang
body: thanks, fixed by add a shared _run_varlen_dense() helper

author: SamitHuang
body:
fixed. logic: 
  1. kernels missing → FLASH_ATTN (unchanged)
  2. GPU < sm_90 → FLASH_ATTN_HUB (new)
  3. GPU >= sm_90 → FLASH_ATTN_3_HUB

author: SamitHuang
body: version=1 / version=2 refer to major API versions of the HF Hub kernel repo (kernels-community/flash-attn2), stored as git branches v1, v2, etc. unrelated to choosing FLASH_ATTN_HUB (FA2) vs FLASH_ATTN_3_HUB (FA3).
